title: dispatcher notifies matches from stale profile hashes
description:
notifications/dispatcher.dispatch_pending picks up any match row with decision='match' and a null notified_at, regardless of which profile_version_hash it was evaluated under. If the user changes their profile (e.g. tightens rent_max_warm via 'flatpilot init'), the dispatcher still sends matches that were valid under the OLD profile's rules and had never been notified. Symptom: user lowers rent cap, next 'flatpilot run' pings Telegram with listings that are over the new cap.

Fix: in dispatch_pending, compute the current profile_version_hash (sha256 of profile.model_dump_json, same logic as matcher.runner._profile_hash) and scope the pending-match query with 'WHERE profile_version_hash = ?'. Old-hash matches stay in the DB as historical records but never fire notifications again.

One-time cleanup on first run after the fix: mark any currently-pending-but-stale-hash matches as notified_at=now without actually sending (they're already orphans). Optional; in practice most users will have already drained them.

Discovered 2026-04-22 when the user's first real profile change (rent_max_warm 1800 -> 900) produced 34 over-limit Telegram pings from the old-hash match queue.
close_reason: Merged in PR #15 (dispatcher profile-hash scoping + stale-match suppression)

title: Dockerfile base image has Python 3.10 but pyproject requires 3.11+
description: The Dockerfile pins mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/python:v1.48.0-jammy, but that image ships Python 3.10.12 and the package requires >=3.11. Build fails with: 'Package flatpilot requires a different Python: 3.10.12 not in >=3.11'. Fix by switching to the -noble tag (Ubuntu 24.04, Python 3.12+) of the same Playwright version.
close_reason: Merged in PR #12 (D-epic PR A: scraper framework + WG-Gesucht anti-bot probe)

title: A7. Dockerfile + docker compose for local/scheduled runs
description:
Optional container path for running FlatPilot without installing Python 3.11+ and Playwright browsers on the host. Phase 5 will add more services (web UI, worker, DB), but for Phase 1 this is a single-service compose.yml that makes the CLI reproducible and schedulable.

Scope:
- Dockerfile based on the official Playwright Python image (Chromium pre-installed with system deps).
- Installs the flatpilot package via pip install -e '.[dev]' so code changes are picked up.
- docker-compose.yml with one service 'flatpilot'.
- Host volume mount for ~/.flatpilot so SQLite DB, sessions, and geocode cache persist across container restarts.
- .env support for TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, SMTP creds, etc.
- .dockerignore to keep the build context small.
- README section documenting: docker compose run --rm flatpilot init, docker compose run --rm flatpilot run, etc.
- Local pip-install path stays working; Docker is an alternative, not a replacement.

title: E3. Radius filter wired into E1
description: Add radius filter to E1 using E2: flat distance <= profile.radius_km, with district allowlist as an optional additional constraint layered on top.

title: C3. stats helpers
description: get_stats() returns: total flats, new-in-last-24h, matched, notified, rejected-by-reason histogram. Used by `flatpilot status` and the dashboard.

title: F. Notifications (Phase 1)
description: Telegram + email; dedup per flat/channel.

title: E1. Deterministic filter module
description: matcher/filters.py: rent band (warm only), rooms band, WBS compatibility (flat requires_wbs -> profile must have wbs=yes with compatible size_category and income_category), district allowlist (if set), household size (flat hints / description), pets (flat pets_allowed vs profile pets), move-in date (flat available_from <= profile.move_in_date + tolerance), furnished pref, min-contract-months. Pure functions, each returns (passes: bool, reason: str|None).

title: D2. WG-Gesucht Wohnungen scraper
description: Parameterized by profile.city + profile.radius_km. Parses search results list + detail page. Extracts all C1 fields. Dedup by WG-Gesucht listing id. Respects the D0 decision on cadence and cookie strategy.

title: E4. `flatpilot match` command + matches writes

title: D. Scraper Framework + WG-Gesucht (Phase 1 MVP)

title: H1. view.py dashboard generator
description: Generates a single HTML file at ~/.flatpilot/dashboard.html. Groups matched flats by decision (Matched / Rejected-this-session / Rejected-historical). Filters: district, rent range (slider), rooms, WBS required y/n. No LLM scores.
close_reason: Merged in PR #10 (H-epic: static HTML dashboard)

title: C2. matches table schema
description: Columns: flat_id (fk), profile_version_hash, decision (match|reject|skipped), decision_reasons_json, decided_at, notified_at (nullable), notified_channels_json. One row per (flat_id, profile_version_hash, decision).

title: G4. Rotating file logging
description: Logs to ~/.flatpilot/logs/flatpilot.log with daily rotation (stdlib RotatingFileHandler or TimedRotatingFileHandler). Console mirror at INFO.

title: D0. Anti-bot survival spike (1 day, blocks D2)
description: Time-boxed investigation: can we poll WG-Gesucht Wohnungen search every 60-120 s for 4 hours without tripping hCaptcha, using Playwright + realistic UA + cookie warm-up? If blocked, enumerate mitigations (lower cadence, longer cookie warm-up, residential IP, RSS-style email fallback) before committing to the scraper design.
notes: Probe run 2026-04-22, Berlin, city_id=8, interval=90s, duration=30min (20 polls), headless=true, Firefox UA, Docker container on arm64 macOS. Results: ok=20 (100.0%), captcha=0, block_http_*=0, block_keyword=0, unknown=0, error_*=0. Consent banner on homepage: auto-accepted via #cmpwelcomebtnyes on first run; not shown on second run (cookie persisted). No soft-block signals observed. Implication for D2: 90s cadence is safe for at least 30 min of sustained polling from a single container/IP. A true 4-hour run (~160 polls) is still recommended before committing D2 to 90s as the default; 120s stays the conservative option if the 4-hour run trips any thresholds. Warm-up (homepage visit + consent) + cookie-state reuse appears sufficient — no headed mode or residential IP needed for this volume.
close_reason: Merged in PR #12 (D-epic PR A: scraper framework + WG-Gesucht anti-bot probe)

Flat coordinates come from (a) scraper if the platform provides them, (b) Nominatim geocode of the address with on-disk cache at ~/.flatpilot/geocode_cache.json (180-day TTL), (c) city/district centroid fallback. Politeness: 1 req/s to Nominatim, custom User-Agent.

Written from scratch, no copy-paste from any other project.

title: FlatPilot MVP — German flat-hunting agent
description: Clean-room, MIT-licensed agent that scrapes German flat listings, deterministically matches them against a user profile, and notifies the user via Telegram / email. Germany-only (MVP), any German city the user configures plus a radius in km. No LLM in Phase 1. Phasing: 0 foundation, 1 MVP (WG-Gesucht + match + notify + HTML dashboard), 2 Kleinanzeigen + cross-platform dedup, 3 inberlinwohnen + apply button + tracking UI, 4 ImmoScout RSS + opt-in auto-apply, 5 multi-user web UI.

title: A4. database.py with thread-local SQLite + forward-migration helper

Written fresh, no lines copied from any other project.

UNIQUE (platform, external_id).

title: A6. doctor command
description: `flatpilot doctor` checks: Python >= 3.11, ~/.flatpilot writable, Playwright browsers present (`playwright install chromium` prompt if not), optional Telegram creds (BOT_TOKEN + CHAT_ID) and SMTP creds in .env. Shows OK / MISSING / optional per check.

title: A. Project Foundation (Phase 0)
description: New clean-room repo scaffolding for FlatPilot. Prereq for every other sub-epic.
close_reason: All child tasks merged; Phase 1 MVP complete

title: L-foundations: `flatpilot login <platform>` CLI for cookie seeding
description:
Headed-browser CLI command that lets the user log in to a rental platform once so the resulting cookies persist to ~/.flatpilot/sessions/<platform>/state.json and every subsequent polite_session(headless=True) call (scrape, dry-run filler, future apply) reuses them.

UX: `flatpilot login wg-gesucht` opens a Playwright Chromium window pointed at the platform's login URL, prints "Log in by hand (including any 2FA / captcha), then press Enter here when you see your dashboard.", waits on stdin, captures storage_state on Enter, closes the window, prints "saved session for <platform> (cookies expire ~<date>)".

Implementation: ~30-50 LOC. Reuses polite_session() with headless=False overridden via a new SessionConfig field or dedicated helper. Per-platform login URL constants live in each platform module (scrapers/wg_gesucht.py, future kleinanzeigen.py etc.) — no per-platform login flow code is needed because the user drives the browser themselves.

Why no credential storage: passwords leaking from ~/.flatpilot would compromise the user's accounts permanently and are commonly reused across services; the Docker runtime can't reach the host OS keyring; programmatic login is a strong anti-bot signal that risks getting accounts flagged. Cookies-only is the safe default. (Discussed in PR #18 hand-off.)

Reuses across every platform we'll ever add (J/K/I/N epics) — one command, all platforms.

Acceptance: `flatpilot login wg-gesucht` opens a window, waits for the user, saves cookies that the L3 dry-run filler can then reuse without raising NotAuthenticatedError.

title: Empirically verify WG-Gesucht contact-form selectors
description: The L3 dry-run filler (FlatPilot-4zkt) ships with provisional CSS selectors for the WG-Gesucht contact form (see fillers/wg_gesucht.py SELECTORS dict). They were chosen from documented form names but have not been validated against the live form, which requires an authenticated session and a real listing URL. First real dry-run will surface mismatches as SelectorMissingError. Resolution: log into wg-gesucht.de via the polite_session (headed mode), navigate to a real listing's contact form, inspect the DOM, update the SELECTORS constants, and re-run the dry-run until every field locates correctly. Blocks L4 because the apply CLI must produce reliable previews before exposing a submit path.
close_reason: Merged in PR #19; selectors verified against live messenger form on two Berlin listings

title: CLI commands don't load ~/.flatpilot/.env (only doctor does)
description:
config.load_env() is only called from the doctor command. notify, run, and scrape never call it, so a user running FlatPilot without Docker will find that TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN / SMTP_* in ~/.flatpilot/.env are ignored — they'd have to export the vars in their shell. With Docker this works because docker-compose.yml's env_file pushes the repo .env into the container, but that's a compose-specific workaround.

Fix: call config.load_env() in the _bootstrap typer callback in src/flatpilot/cli.py so every command loads the app-dir .env at startup, regardless of invocation path.

Minor follow-up to the F-epic; noticed while wiring up the first Telegram notification on 2026-04-22.

title: I-bis-1. Address normalization + fuzzy match
description: Normalize address (lowercase, strip punctuation, 'Straße' <-> 'Str.'). Fuzzy match key = (normalized_address, rent_warm +- 50 EUR, size +- 3 qm). Store canonical_flat_id on flats; keep all source listing URLs.

title: I-bis. Cross-platform dedup (Phase 2)
description: Same flat posted on WG-Gesucht AND Kleinanzeigen should produce ONE notification. Triggered when a second scraper lands.
close_reason: All I-bis tasks complete — dedup stamped at ingest (FlatPilot-0ktm, PR #21); matcher+notifier read canonical (FlatPilot-k40y, PR #22)

title: I1. Kleinanzeigen Wohnung-miete scraper
description: Search Wohnung category with city + radius, parse price / Warm-vs-Kaltmiete disambiguation, Zimmer, qm, Stadtteil. Same output shape as D2.
notes:
Kleinanzeigen anti-bot D0 probe (2026-04-22)
--------------------------------------------
Result: 158 / 158 polls = 100% ok over ~4 h at 90 s cadence. Zero
Cloudflare challenges (Turnstile / 'Just a moment' interstitial), zero
captchas, zero HTTP >=400, zero block_keyword hits, zero error_* events.

Setup
- Script: scripts/kleinanzeigen_probe.py (FlatPilot-3hu2 scaffolding)
- URL: https://www.kleinanzeigen.de/s-wohnung-mieten/berlin/c203l3331
- User-agent: Firefox 121 Linux (same as wg_probe)
- Headless Chromium in the flatpilot Docker container
- Storage state: ~/.flatpilot/sessions/kleinanzeigen/state.json
  (warmed up once, reused across the run)
- Cadence: 90 s between polls, 4 h total, no jitter

Timeline
- Smoke (2026-04-22 13:16-13:45 Berlin): 20/20 ok
- Full D0 (2026-04-22 13:46-17:45 Berlin): 158/158 ok
- Cumulative: 178/178 ok across ~4.5 h of contiguous polling with a
  shared session — no degradation late in the run.

Design implications for the scraper (FlatPilot-3hu2 itself)
- 90-120 s cadence is safe for Berlin Wohnung-mieten. No stealth flags
  needed up front; we do not need randomized intervals to start.
- 'ok' detection can stay selector-based in the scraper even though the
  probe used a generic heuristic — the probe's job was only anti-bot.
  Next step is to capture a real search page and identify the listing
  card selector (kleinanzeigen uses .aditem as of recent history; must
  verify empirically).
- Cookie warm-up matches wg_gesucht: load homepage, accept consent
  banner if present. Consent banner was not shown in the probe run
  (likely because the UA + no prior cookies leads Kleinanzeigen to
  skip the CMP for this session, or the selectors missed a hidden
  variant — re-verify when building the scraper).
- Session state persists correctly via docker-compose's
  ${FLATPILOT_DATA_DIR:-${HOME}/.flatpilot}:/root/.flatpilot mount
  (verified by state.json surviving across runs).

Still unknown (follow-up work in FlatPilot-6hix, not this task)
- Does Kleinanzeigen challenge on detail-page navigation (clicking into
  a listing)? Probe only hit the search URL.
- Per-listing rate limits? Probe did not fetch individual flats.
- Behaviour from a different egress IP (e.g. CI / cloud). The probe ran
  from the author's home ISP; a VPN or datacenter IP may trip
  Cloudflare where this one did not.

title: Paginate inberlinwohnen.de Wohnungsfinder beyond page 1
description:
The Phase 1 inberlinwohnen scraper (FlatPilot-rqks) reads only page 1 of https://inberlinwohnen.de/wohnungsfinder/ — ~10 listings per pass. The full listing today is ~216 entries across 22 numbered pages. This is fine for steady-state polling (new listings surface on page 1) but on first install we miss the older inventory.

Implementation: identify the pagination URL pattern (server-rendered or Livewire-update? probe with curl or a manual scrape), extend fetch_new to walk pages until either (a) all hits already exist in the flats table or (b) a configurable cap (e.g. 5 pages) is reached. Respect a per-page polite delay; the existing polite_session covers per-fetch headers.

Acceptance: a single 'flatpilot scrape --platform inberlinwohnen' run on a fresh install ingests >100 listings (assuming the page count is ~22 today), and steady-state polling still costs only ~1 page per pass.

title: doctor: per-platform cookie-expiry check
description:
Add a doctor check per registered platform that probes a known logged-in-only URL and classifies the response as OK / EXPIRED / NEVER_LOGGED_IN. Surfaces "your wg-gesucht session expires in N days, run `flatpilot login wg-gesucht`" at `flatpilot doctor` time so users discover expiry proactively, not at the moment an apply attempt blows up at midnight.

Implementation: per-platform constant for the canary URL (e.g. WG-Gesucht's /mein-wg-gesucht.html). The check uses polite_session() in headless mode, navigates, and checks for a login redirect (existing LOGIN_URL_FRAGMENTS pattern from fillers/wg_gesucht.py) plus an optional "logged in" DOM marker. Cookie expiry timestamps come from the storage_state JSON.

Status mapping into the existing doctor table:
- OK -> green, 'session valid until <date>'
- EXPIRED -> yellow optional, 'run `flatpilot login <platform>`'
- NEVER_LOGGED_IN -> yellow optional, 'no session yet — run `flatpilot login <platform>` if you want to apply via this platform'

Acceptance: `flatpilot doctor` shows a row per registered platform with its session status; expiry warnings fire at least 3 days before the actual cookies expire.

title: doctor's Telegram check looks for TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID in env, but chat_id lives in profile.json
description:
doctor.py:_check_telegram reports 'optional / missing: TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID' when TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID is not an environment variable. But FlatPilot's design stores chat_id in profile.json (notifications.telegram.chat_id) — only the bot token is env-based (via profile.notifications.telegram.bot_token_env, default TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN). The check is therefore misleading: a user with a correctly configured profile still sees 'missing chat_id'.

Fix: rewrite _check_telegram to:
 1. Try to load_profile(); if no profile, report 'profile not set — run init'.
 2. If profile.notifications.telegram.enabled, check os.environ for profile.notifications.telegram.bot_token_env and for profile.notifications.telegram.chat_id being non-empty.
 3. If not enabled, report 'disabled in profile' (no missing-var nag).
Similarly for _check_smtp: only warn about SMTP_* when profile.notifications.email.enabled.

Noticed 2026-04-22 while onboarding the user's real profile post-Phase-1 merge. Purely cosmetic — no functional bug.

title: N. Opt-in Auto-Apply with safety rails (Phase 4)
description: Per-saved-search auto-apply; daily caps, cooldowns, pause switch.

title: K1. RSS parser adapter
description: Consume feedparser output. Map RSS items into Flat records. Limited fields available from RSS -> optional detail-page enrichment pass behind a flag.

title: N2. Daily cap per platform
description: Default 20/day; enforced by querying applications.

title: L. Per-flat Apply Button (Phase 3)
description: CLI + UI button to contact the landlord for a specific flat. Starts with WG-Gesucht contact form. Per-site Playwright fillers.
close_reason: Epic L (Per-flat Apply Button, Phase 3) fully closed. Final leaf L4 shipped in PR #24; L1/L2/L3/L5 closed earlier.

title: J. inberlinwohnen.de scraper (Phase 3)
description: Berlin municipal housing scraper. Opt-in: only runs if profile.city == 'Berlin'. WBS-heavy.

title: N1. Saved-search schema with auto_apply toggle
description: Profile gains a saved_searches list, each with its own filters overlay and an auto_apply: bool.

title: N5. Global PAUSE switch
description: Presence of ~/.flatpilot/PAUSE halts all auto-apply immediately. Mention in doctor.

title: M3. Applied tab with status badges
description: Shows applications rows sorted by applied_at; status badges; filter by status.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #24 (feat/lm-apply-and-tracking-ui). 9 tasks across L4 apply CLI + M-series tracking UI. 144 tests passing.

title: L2. Anschreiben template composer
description: Template-based Anschreiben per platform. User maintains templates at ~/.flatpilot/templates/<platform>.md. Fills profile placeholders. AI-generated variants are explicitly out of scope for Phase 3.

title: J1. inberlinwohnen Wohnungen scraper
description: Parse listings. Flag requires_wbs=True by default (most are). Extract WBS size + income constraints when stated.

title: N4. Skip-and-log on missing field/attachment
description: Auto-apply never submits incomplete applications. Failed attempts log with reason and are visible in the Applied tab.

title: L1. applications table schema
description: Columns: flat_id, platform, listing_url, title, rent_warm, rooms, size_sqm, district, applied_at, method (manual|auto), message_sent (text), attachments_sent_json, status (submitted|failed|viewing_invited|rejected|no_response), response_received_at, response_text, notes.

title: O. 'Connected accounts' page with per-platform Connect buttons
description:
Web UI page (under Epic O — FastAPI + React/Next.js) that lists every supported rental platform with a Connect / Reconnect button. Clicking Connect spawns a Playwright headed browser window pointed at the platform's login URL, the user logs in inside that controlled window (entering credentials directly into the platform's site — FlatPilot never sees them), the page detects login completion (URL leaves /login OR user clicks "Done"), captures cookies via storage_state(), saves them to ~/.flatpilot/sessions/<platform>/state.json, closes the window, and the UI updates to show the connection as active with an expiry date.

Why this UX framing rather than a literal OAuth-style redirect: WG-Gesucht / Kleinanzeigen / ImmoScout24 don't publish OAuth endpoints. There is no protocol-level way to make "sign in with the platform → callback to FlatPilot" work. The Playwright-controlled window is the workaround that delivers the same UX without requiring platform cooperation.

Backend: identical to the `flatpilot login <platform>` CLI command. This task is the UI layer on top of that engine — the headed-browser launching, cookie capture, and storage paths are shared. So the task here is mostly:
- Connections page React component (logo + status + Connect/Reconnect button per platform)
- FastAPI endpoint POST /connect/<platform> that spawns the browser via the same backend the CLI uses
- WebSocket or polling for the UI to learn when the connection completes

UI mockup:

  Connected accounts:
    ☑ WG-Gesucht        Connected · expires Mar 22  [Reconnect]
    □ Kleinanzeigen     Not connected               [Connect →]
    □ ImmoScout24       Not connected               [Connect →]

Depends on: Epic O having basic structure (FastAPI backend + React frontend scaffolding). Depends on the `flatpilot login` CLI shipping first because it owns the underlying mechanism.

Acceptance: from the deployed Web UI, clicking a Connect button opens a real headed browser window, lets the user log in to the actual rental platform, and on completion the UI shows the platform as connected without ever transmitting the user's password to FlatPilot.

title: Set up pytest suite + initial test coverage
description:
Why this issue exists
---------------------
CLAUDE.md lists pytest as the test framework ('focus on matcher logic
and scraper parsers with fixture HTML'), but the repo has no tests/
directory. Every change through Phase 1 was validated by manual
Docker smoke runs only. That worked for a one-person project at MVP
stage, but as the codebase grows (I-epic Kleinanzeigen, I-bis dedup,
L-epic Apply flow, N-epic Auto-Apply with safety rails) the surface
area that can silently regress is growing fast.

Noticed while shipping FlatPilot-knf (PR #16): a symmetric code path
I didn't exercise manually (SMTP 'enabled but missing') shipped
without coverage because there was no fast way to assert the
branches. Also introduced a subtle regression window — doctor now
calls load_profile() and will crash on a malformed profile.json — that
a single unit test would have caught at author time.

What needs to be done
---------------------
Minimum viable scope (split into sub-tasks if the work is >1 PR):

1. Infrastructure
   - Create tests/ with pytest config in pyproject.toml
   - conftest.py with fixtures for a scratch FLATPILOT_DIR (tmp_path-based)
     and an example Profile (leveraging Profile.load_example())
   - Update pyproject 'dev' extras to include pytest, pytest-cov; wire
     up 'ruff + pytest' as the quality gate used by CI and the README
     Build-and-Test block

2. Initial coverage targets (in priority order)
   - matcher/filters.py — deterministic filters are the domain core;
     every filter should have edge-case unit tests (rent band, rooms,
     WBS, distance, furnished_pref, contract length, pets, smoker)
   - matcher/distance.py — Haversine + Nominatim adapter (geocoding
     cache hit/miss), with httpx mock
   - scrapers/wg_gesucht.py — parse() against saved fixture HTML
     (resources/tests/wg_gesucht/*.html); covers city-ID lookup,
     selector resilience, URL construction
   - doctor.py — all branches of _check_telegram / _check_smtp
     (including malformed profile)
   - notifications/dispatcher.py — the profile-hash scoping logic
     introduced in FlatPilot-usm

3. CI
   - Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs ruff + pytest on every PR
   - Fail the workflow on test failure or < some floor coverage
     (e.g. 60% to start, raise over time)

Why P4
------
Phase 1 is live and working. Tests are an investment in velocity, not
a fix for a user-visible bug. File now so we can pick it up between
feature epics (good 'cooldown' work) rather than forgetting the gap.

title: O. Web UI (multi-user, Phase 5)
description: FastAPI backend + React/Next.js frontend. Per-user profiles, magic-link auth, SQLite -> Postgres migration path.

title: O3. React/Next.js tabs
description: Port Matches / Applied / Responses tabs from the HTML dashboard.

title: O4. Docker-compose + deployment docs
description: One-command local stack; deployment instructions for a VPS.

title: O1. Architecture decision record
description: Decide: FastAPI + Next.js; magic-link auth; SQLite -> Postgres migration path; deployment story (docker-compose starter).

title: O2. Per-user isolation in data model
description: All tables gain user_id; row-level scoping; migration from single-user state.

value: Never push directly to main. Every change goes through a PR: git fetch origin; git checkout -b <type>/<slug> origin/main; commit; git push -u origin <branch>; gh pr create --base main --head <branch> --fill. Return the PR URL and stop. Force-push allowed only on personal feature branches that nobody else has pulled (--force-with-lease).

Clean-room policy: FlatPilot is independent MIT-licensed. Never copy code from any other project (including any job-hunting agent that may have inspired the high-level architecture). Read outside code only as a design reference; always rewrite from scratch.

For FlatPilot: one PR per epic. If an epic has >10 tasks, split into multiple PRs (thematic split, topologically ordered). Otherwise the whole epic lands in one PR — one commit per task, each commit message referencing its beads ID. Small leftover tasks from completed sub-areas can ride along if they fit the theme. Why: the user set this rule after observing that per-task PRs were noise and mixed-theme mega-PRs were hard to review. How to apply: before claiming the first task in an epic, count the epic's open tasks with 'bd list' filtered to that epic; if >10, decide on a thematic split (e.g. 'matcher core' vs 'matcher CLI') and declare it in the first PR description. Each PR closes every beads task it contains.

Never store user passwords for rental platforms (WG-Gesucht, Kleinanzeigen, ImmoScout24, future) anywhere in FlatPilot — not in profile.json, .env, or any other file. Reasons: (1) Docker container can't reach host OS keyring, leaving only file storage which is unsafe for credentials; (2) programmatic credential login is a strong anti-bot signal that risks getting accounts flagged; (3) 2FA / captchas still require human intervention even with stored passwords; (4) ToS posture is much worse than cookie reuse from a real human session. Solution: cookies-only auth. CLI version (FlatPilot-5b8) uses headed Playwright + 'press Enter when logged in' UX. Future multi-user Web UI (FlatPilot-8jx) uses browser extension or bookmarklet to capture cookies from the user's normal browser session — never asks for or stores passwords. None of WG-Gesucht / Kleinanzeigen / ImmoScout24 publish OAuth endpoints, so a literal 'Sign in with Platform' redirect-and-callback flow is structurally impossible. Decided in PR #18 session 2026-04-22.

FlatPilot is currently single-user CLI (Phase 3). Long-term direction: convert to small multi-user web app — 'Option C' scope — for friends-of-friends, no public marketing, no payments. Strict sequencing: complete Phase 3 CLI for the developer's own use FIRST, then validate by using it personally on real WG-Gesucht searches for 1-2 weeks, THEN start Epic O (Web UI / FlatPilot-0wfb). Do NOT start FastAPI / React / Postgres work until Phase 3 is shipped end-to-end and personally validated. The CLI work is not throwaway — every Python module becomes a backend module the FastAPI app calls later. Phase 5 will need: Hetzner/Fly hosting, Postgres migration, FastAPI backend, React frontend, magic-link email auth, browser extension OR bookmarklet for per-user platform cookie capture. Decided in PR #18 session 2026-04-22.
No AI co-author trailers in commits. No 'Co-Authored-By: Claude ...', no '🤖 Generated with Claude Code'. Commits are authored by the human only.

Commit author for this repo: Mukhammad Ibrokhimov <ibrohimovmuhammad2020@gmail.com>. In a fresh clone: git config user.email ibrohimovmuhammad2020@gmail.com && git config user.name 'Mukhammad Ibrokhimov'. Any other email on the machine is NOT for commits here.

After a FlatPilot PR merges, DO NOT delete the local or remote feature branch, and DO NOT drop stashes, unless the user explicitly asks. Why: user's standing preference to preserve merged branches for historical reference (e.g. feat/l2-anschreiben-composer was called out as hands-off in the handoff). How to apply: after merge, only sync main (git checkout main && git pull --ff-only) and stop. Do not run 'git branch -d', 'git push --delete', 'git stash drop', or any similar cleanup without an explicit request. Treat 'cleanup' as scope the user opts into, not a default step.
